majors.count_method: official Princeton Admission Degrees & Departments page states students can choose from among 37 concentrations
titles: Public Policy (Princeton School of Public and International Affairs)
majors.notes: official Princeton Admission Degrees & Departments page states students can choose from among 37 concentrations; titles extracted from official page: https://admission.princeton.edu/academics/degrees-departments

admissions.testing_policy: Princeton is test-optional for first-year applicants seeking fall 2026 or fall 2027 entry, and it will return to requiring the SAT or ACT for applicants seeking fall 2028 entry.
admissions.gpa_policy: Princeton does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official pages and instead frames evaluation around readiness for its rigorous academic environment in the context of available school opportunities.
admissions.course_rigor: Princeton presents a suggested course of study rather than rigid requirements: if possible, students should complete four years of English, four years of math, four years of one language, at least two years of laboratory science, and at least two years of history while taking the most rigorous available courses.
admissions.recommendations: Princeton requires a counselor recommendation and two teacher recommendations from higher-level courses in different core academic areas.
admissions.essays: Princeton requires Princeton-specific questions and a graded written paper, preferably in English or history.

projects_research: Princeton describes itself as a research institution that also prides itself on its liberal arts curriculum.

evidence.claim: Princeton is test-optional for first-year applicants applying for fall 2026 and fall 2027 entry; Princeton says it will once again require testing for students applying for fall 2028 entry.
evidence.source_excerpt: Our test optional policy continues to be in place for first-year applicants applying for fall 2026 and fall 2027 entry. We will once again require testing for students applying for fall 2028 entry.

evidence.claim: Princeton requires a counselor recommendation plus two teacher recommendations from higher level courses in different academic areas.
evidence.source_excerpt: Counselor Recommendation ... Two (2) Teacher Recommendations. Please ask two of your teachers who have taught you in higher level courses ... in different academic areas of study.

evidence.claim: Princeton requires Princeton-specific questions and a graded written paper for first-year applicants.
evidence.source_excerpt: Princeton University requires you to answer Princeton-specific questions ... Princeton also requires you to submit a graded written paper as part of your application.

evidence.claim: Princeton describes itself as a research institution that also prides itself on its liberal arts curriculum.
evidence.source_excerpt: As a research institution that also prides itself on its liberal arts curriculum, Princeton allows students to explore areas across the humanities and the arts, the natural sciences, and the social sciences.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Princeton University' (legacyId=780) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 6 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

African American Studies; Anthropology; Architecture; Art & Archaeology; Astrophysical Sciences; Chemical and Biological Engineering; Chemistry; Civil and Environmental Engineering; Classics; Comparative Literature; Computer Science (A.B.); Computer Science (B.S.E.)

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (37 titles).
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Princeton University' (legacyId=780) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Princeton Alumni story pages, prioritizing person-specific profiles with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation impact described on official Princeton Alumni pages.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Princeton Alumni story pages on alumni.princeton.edu.

items.name: Fei-Fei Li
items.bio: Princeton Alumni describes Fei-Fei Li ’99 as the Sequoia Capital Professor in Computer Science at Stanford University and co-director of Stanford HAI, highlighting more than 25 years of work advancing AI, computer vision, deep learning, and healthcare AI systems.
items.description: Stanford computer scientist and AI leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: John Fitzpatrick
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says John Fitzpatrick *78 helped lead ornithology to new heights and identifies him as the force behind the citizen-science platform eBird.
items.description: Ornithologist and eBird leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit graduate-alumni class-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Mimi Onuoha
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says media artist and researcher Mimi Onuoha ’11 examines how technology shapes culture and behavior, especially how lived experiences are converted into data and represented or misrepresented.
items.description: Media artist and researcher focused on data and technology.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Jordan Salama
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Jordan Salama ’19, after publishing his second book, was set to lead a Princeton Journeys trip to Buenos Aires designed for recent alumni.
items.description: Author and Princeton Journeys trip leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation summary.

items.name: Daniella Cohen
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Daniella Cohen ’22 co-founded GIVE, a nonprofit that connects students worldwide through pen-pal writing exchanges, and highlights her involvement with the Princeton Prize in Race Relations.
items.description: Nonprofit co-founder and Princeton Prize in Race Relations volunteer.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation summary.

items.name: Lou Chen
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Lou Chen ’19 helped grow a volunteer violin-lesson effort into a broader Trenton arts and music program connecting Princeton and Trenton communities.
items.description: Community arts organizer and Trenton music-program builder.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation summary.

items.name: Karthick Ramakrishnan
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Karthick Ramakrishnan *02, president of the Association of Princeton Graduate Alumni, works to ensure graduate students and graduate alumni are recognized contributors to Princeton’s excellence.
items.description: Association of Princeton Graduate Alumni president and graduate-alumni advocate.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit graduate-alumni class-year evidence and substantive role summary.

items.name: Mary Newburn
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Mary Newburn ’97 served as president of the Alumni Association and chair of the Alumni Council, emphasizing belonging and community-building among Tigers.
items.description: Former Princeton Alumni Association president and Alumni Council chair.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Monica Moore Thompson
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Monica Moore Thompson ’89 became president of the Alumni Association and chair of the Alumni Council, with a leadership focus on unity across the alumni community.
items.description: Princeton Alumni Association president and Alumni Council chair.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Henry Von Kohorn
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Henry Von Kohorn ’66 founded the Princeton Prize in Race Relations to honor high school students advancing racial equity and to serve the nation through Princeton’s outreach.
items.description: Founder of the Princeton Prize in Race Relations.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: David Foster
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says David Foster ’01 views establishing a scholarship as a ‘triple win’ and highlights his deepening appreciation for the Princeton alumni experience across life stages.
items.description: Alumni donor and scholarship benefactor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive alumni-engagement summary.

items.name: Adam Lichtenstein
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Adam Lichtenstein ’95 *10 returned to earn a master’s in finance and later served in several Alumni Council leadership roles, including treasurer.
items.description: Finance alumnus and Alumni Council treasurer.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit undergraduate and graduate class-year evidence plus substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Eric Plummer
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Eric Plummer ’10 sees the Princeton connection as life-changing and has been especially involved in recent-alumni engagement through the Alumni Council.
items.description: Alumni Council leader focused on recent alumni engagement.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Ryan Ruskin
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Ryan Ruskin ’90 has remained deeply involved with Princeton for decades and describes his long-term commitment to the University community.
items.description: Longtime Princeton alumni volunteer and community leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive alumni-engagement summary.

items.name: Steven Marcus
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Steven Marcus ’10, chair of the Princeton Prize in Race Relations, is inspired by both teen winners and alumni volunteers working on racial equity.
items.description: Chair of the Princeton Prize in Race Relations.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Melissa Zdrodowski
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Melissa Zdrodowski ’94 uses her communications and technology skills to help bring generations of Tigers together through Alumni Council service.
items.description: Alumni communications and technology leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Myles Cohen
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Myles Cohen ’63 established a scholarship as an accelerated expression of gratitude for the aid he once received from Princeton.
items.description: Scholarship donor and member of the 1746 Society.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive alumni-impact summary.

items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Jacklyn Boney Bruce ’99 credits Princeton with giving her confidence to pursue her interests and now wants future students to have the same opportunity.
items.description: 1746 Society member and future-focused alumni donor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Princeton Alumni person-specific feature page with explicit class-year evidence and substantive alumni-impact summary.

items.name: Bliss Freytag Smith
items.bio: Princeton Alumni says Bliss Freytag Smith ’87, vice chair of the Committee on Regional Associations, sees Princeton as a continuing source of community, friendship, and opportunity.
